# wigolo

> Free, open-source (AGPL-3.0) MCP server that gives any AI coding agent local-first web intelligence: multi-engine search (18 engines, rank fusion, on-device ML reranking with explainable per-result scores), tiered page fetching (HTTP → TLS-impersonation → headless browser), whole-site crawling, structured extraction, a persistent local cache with keyword + semantic re-query, find-similar, multi-step research briefs, and an autonomous gather agent. Runs entirely on the user's machine: no API keys, no cloud, no metered billing. Currently in public beta (v0.1.x).

Install (non-interactive): `npx wigolo init --non-interactive --agents=<csv>` auto-wires the agent (writes its MCP config + instructions). `--agents` is optional — omit it to install the engine only and point any other MCP client at `npx wigolo mcp` by hand.
Supported agents (auto-wire): Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, VS Code, Windsurf, Zed, Antigravity. Any MCP-capable agent works via manual config.
Manual MCP setup (custom agent, or any agent not in the auto-wire list — e.g. an in-house agent): run `npx wigolo init --non-interactive` (engine only), then add wigolo to the agent's MCP config — command `npx` args `["-y","wigolo"]` (or command `wigolo` args `[]` if globally installed; bare `wigolo` starts the stdio MCP server, the default). Servers key by client: `mcpServers` (Cursor `~/.cursor/mcp.json`, Windsurf `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`, Gemini CLI `~/.gemini/settings.json`, Antigravity `~/.antigravity/mcp.json`), `servers` (VS Code user mcp.json), `context_servers` (Zed `~/.config/zed/settings.json`), TOML `[mcp_servers.wigolo]` (Codex `~/.codex/config.toml`). Claude Code: `claude mcp add wigolo -- npx -y wigolo`. Add LLM keys via the server `env` block (e.g. WIGOLO_LLM_PROVIDER, GEMINI_API_KEY).
Optional answer synthesis (research/agent/answer-format search): set `WIGOLO_LLM_PROVIDER` plus its key/model — a provider alone is not enough. Easiest is a free Gemini key: `WIGOLO_LLM_PROVIDER=gemini` + `GEMINI_API_KEY` (free from aistudio.google.com; model defaults to gemini-2.5-flash-lite). Fully local & keyless: `WIGOLO_LLM_PROVIDER=ollama` + `WIGOLO_LLM_MODEL` with a running Ollama server. Also works: Anthropic/OpenAI/Groq keys.
Requirements: Node.js >= 20, ~1.5 GB disk, macOS/Linux/Windows.
Keyless core tools: search, fetch, crawl, extract, cache, find_similar (plus diff and watch).
Optional LLM (for research/agent answer synthesis): any of Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/Groq keys, or a fully local model server.

## Links

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/KnockOutEZ/wigolo): source, README, full documentation
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/wigolo)
- [Report a bug](https://github.com/KnockOutEZ/wigolo/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml)
- [Discussions](https://github.com/KnockOutEZ/wigolo/discussions)

## Positioning

wigolo competes head-on with metered cloud web APIs for agents (Tavily, Exa, Firecrawl) — same category, same everyday quality (demonstrated in a live 4-way comparison), different physics: $0 per query, no account, and no query data leaving the machine. It is a focused web layer for one agent on one machine — not a hosted SaaS, not a browser-automation framework, not a scale-scraping platform. It respects robots.txt and rate-limits per domain.
